pageSummary: Below is a summary of your pension choices. Review your choices closely and make any needed changes. When you are satisfied with your pension choices, submit them.

changeDateBeneModalContent.heading: Do you want to change your choices?
changeDateBeneModalContent.introContent: Youll need to start the retirement process again if you change one or more of these choices:
changeDateBeneModalContent.listedContent:
• Your last day of employment
  • Date you begin receiving benefits
  • Your beneficiaries
changeDateBeneModalContent.endingContent: This is because the benefits options available and amounts may change, too.

retirementDatesContent.heading: Your Retirement Dates
retirementDatesContent.changeButtonAltText: Select this button to change your retirement dates.
retirementDatesContent.beginBenefitDateLabel: Date You May Begin Receiving Benefits

paymentElectionBenefits.paymentOptionDescription: Level income joint survivor pop up

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you only, until you die
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• No payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (for payments after your death)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die, then 50 1/3% of your monthly payment to your beneficiary until your beneficiary dies
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• After you die, 50 1/3% benefit payment to your beneficiary until he or she dies
  • If married and beneficiary is not your spouse, your spouse must consent
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (for payments after your death)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die (which increases, if your beneficiary dies before you), then 50 1/3% of your monthly payment to your beneficiary until your beneficiary dies
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• After you die, 50 1/3% benefit payment to your beneficiary until he or she dies
  • If married and beneficiary is not your spouse, your spouse must consent
  • If beneficiary dies before you, the amount paid to you increases
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormToYouAmountList.popUpAmountText: If your beneficiary dies before you, your payments will increase to $116.32.

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (for payments after your death)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die, then to your beneficiary until your beneficiary dies
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• If you die before 10-year cutoff date, your monthly payments will continue to your beneficiary until the 10-year cutoff date, or until your beneficiary dies
  • If you die after 10-year cutoff date, no payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • If married and beneficiary is not your spouse, your spouse must consent
  • Beneficiary can be added after you retire
  • If no beneficiary when you die, remaining payments made per plan rules
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments before May 1, 2019)
Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments on or after May 1, 2019)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die, then to a beneficiary, with an increased benefit until you reach (or would have reached) age 62, when your payment is reduced (because you can take or could have taken your Social Security benefit)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• Receive retirement payments immediately if you retire early (between ages 55 and 62)
  • Increased benefit until you reach age 62 (and until you are eligible to start your Social Security benefit)
  • Reduced benefit when you reach age 62 (because you can take your Social Security benefit)
  • If you die before age 62, beneficiary receives 50 1/3% of your payment, until you would have reached age 62 (when payment will be reduced, as it would have if you were still living)
  • If you die after age 62, 50 1/3% of what you would have received if you had chosen the regular 50 1/3% Joint and Survivor payment option will be paid to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments before May 1, 2019)
Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments on or after May 1, 2019)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelHeader: Level income joint survivor pop up
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die (which increases, if your beneficiary dies before you), then to a beneficiary, with an increased benefit until you reach (or would have reached) age 62, when the payment is reduced (because you can take or could have taken your Social Security benefit)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• Receive retirement payments immediately if you retire early (between ages 55 and 62)
  • Increased benefit until you reach age 62 (and until you are eligible to start your Social Security benefit)
  • Reduced benefit when you reach age 62 (because you can take your Social Security benefit)
  • If you die before age 62, beneficiary receives 50 1/3% of your payment, until you would have reached age 62 (when payment will be reduced, as it would have if you were still living)
  • If you die after age 62, 50 1/3% of what you would have received if you had chosen the regular 100% Joint and Survivor payment option will be paid to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • If beneficiary dies before you, the amount paid to you increases
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormToYouAmountList.popUpAmountText: If your beneficiary dies before you, your payments will increase to $7,324.32.
If your beneficiary dies before you, your payments will increase to $7,324.32.

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments before May 1, 2019)
Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments on or after May 1, 2019)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you only, until you die, with an increased benefit until you reach age 62, when your payment is reduced (because you can take your Social Security benefit)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• Receive retirement payments immediately if you retire early (between ages 55 and 62)
  • No payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • Increased benefit until you reach age 62 (and until you are eligible to start your Social Security benefit)
  • Reduced benefit when you reach age 62 (because you can take your Social Security benefit)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments before May 1, 2019)
Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments on or after May 1, 2019)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until the cutoff date (May 1, 2020)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• If you die before 10-year cutoff date, your monthly payments will continue to your beneficiary until the 10-year cutoff date
  • If you die after 10-year cutoff date, no payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • Beneficiary can be added after you retire
  • Contingent beneficiary can be named (in case your beneficiary dies before the 10-year cutoff date)
  • If no beneficiary when you die, remaining payments made per plan rules
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments before May 1, 2020)
Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments on or after May 1, 2020)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die, then your monthly payment to your beneficiary until the cutoff date (May 1, 2020)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• If you die before 10-year cutoff date, your monthly payments will continue to your beneficiary until the 10-year cutoff date
  • If you die after 10-year cutoff date, no payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • Beneficiary can be added after you retire
  • Contingent beneficiary can be named (in case your beneficiary dies before the 10-year cutoff date)
  • If no beneficiary when you die, remaining payments made per plan rules

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments before May 1, 2020)
Beneficiary Benefit (after your death, for payments on or after May 1, 2020)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A one-time payment to you only
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• No payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • Entire value of your pension benefit (or, in some cases, a portion of the entire value)
  • Subject to taxation, and the Internal Revenue Service requires 20% be automatically withheld
  • May be subject to 10% penalty tax on early distributions
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

submitContentIntro: When you Submit, you acknowledge that you've read and understand Your Notice of Rights and that your choices may result in the waiver of the applicable
submitContentIntroTipRollover: By law, you have at least 30 days to think about your pension choices and decide if you want to consent to receiving benefits.After you start your benefit, you can't change the payment option. You can waive your right to this 30-day period by choosing Submit below. If you're not ready now to consent to receiving benefits, do not submit your choices.You can choose Come Back Later and return to this site when you're ready to submit your pension choices.
submitContentNote: Note: After you submit your pension choices, your authorization is required to finalize them.

quitModalContent.title: What do you want to do?

bodyList.heading: Save
bodyList.paragraph: Keep the choices you've made, so far. Finish making pension choices later.

bodyList.heading: Quit
bodyList.paragraph: Cancel your pension request and lose all the pension choices you've made, so far.

bodyList.heading: Return
bodyList.paragraph: Go back to the current page without quitting.
